duckdb/duckdb#18225.body:
### What happens?

Howdy!

I'm working on adding async/threaded support to Llama Index for DuckDB.

We're using asyncio.to_thread to concurrently read/write to DuckDB from multiple threads, using `.cursor()` to ensure a dedicated per-thread client.

We're seeing deadlocks when doing inserts from Arrow tables at the same time as reads:

Here's the [insert code](https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index/blob/6095658d600be307db38bc50ac2297354332dc09/llama-index-integrations/storage/kvstore/llama-index-storage-kvstore-duckdb/llama_index/storage/kvstore/duckdb/base.py#L231-L239).

This deadlocks:
```
        arrow_table = pyarrow.Table.from_pylist(rows)


        _ = self.client.from_arrow(arrow_table).query(
            "put_all",
            sql_query=f"""
            INSERT OR REPLACE INTO {self.table.alias}
            SELECT * from put_all;
            """,
        )
```

This does not deadlock:
```
        arrow_table = pyarrow.Table.from_pylist(rows)

        _ = self.client.sql(
            query=f"""
            INSERT OR REPLACE INTO {self.table.alias}
            SELECT * from arrow_table;
            """,
        )
```

In another section I'm doing:
`self.client.from_arrow(arrow_table).insert_into(self.table.alias)` which also does not appear to deadlock

Any thoughts?

### To Reproduce

```

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_async_deadlock(self, kv_store: DuckDBKVStore):
        key = "id_1"
        value = {"name": "John Doe", "text": "Hello, world!"}

        def generate_kv_pairs(prefix: str, count: int):
            return [(f"{prefix}_{i}", value) for i in range(count)]

        tasks = [
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("1", 1000)),
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("2", 1000)),
            kv_store.aget_all(),
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("3", 1000)),
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("4", 1000)),
        ]

        _ = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)

        assert not any(isinstance(task, Exception) for task in tasks)

        tasks = [
            kv_store.aget_all(),
            kv_store.aget_all(),
            kv_store.aget(key="1_1"),
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("5", 1000), collection="test"),
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("6", 1000), collection="test"),
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("7", 1000), collection="test"),
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("8", 1000), collection="test"),
            kv_store.aget_all(),
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("9", 1000)),
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("10", 1000)),
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("11", 1000)),
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("12", 1000)),
        ]

        _ = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)

        assert not any(isinstance(task, Exception) for task in tasks)


    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_async_deadlock(self, kv_store: DuckDBKVStore):
        key = "id_1"
        value = {"name": "John Doe", "text": "Hello, world!"}

        kv_store_2 = DuckDBKVStore(client=kv_store.client)

        def generate_kv_pairs(prefix: str, count: int):
            return [(f"{prefix}_{i}", value) for i in range(count)]

        tasks = [
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("1", 1000)),
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("2", 1000)),
            kv_store.aget_all(),
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("3", 1000)),
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("4", 1000)),
            kv_store_2.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("1", 1000)),
            kv_store_2.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("2", 1000)),
            kv_store_2.aget_all(),
            kv_store_2.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("3", 1000)),
            kv_store_2.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("4", 1000)),
        ]

        _ = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)

        assert not any(isinstance(task, Exception) for task in tasks)

        async def get_all_keys_individually(kv_store: DuckDBKVStore):
            keys = await kv_store.aget_all()
            for key in list(keys.keys()):
                #print(key)
                await kv_store.aget(key)
            return keys

        tasks = [
            kv_store.aget_all(),
            kv_store.aget_all(),
            kv_store.aget(key="1_1"),
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("5", 1000), collection="test"),
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("6", 1000), collection="test"),
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("7", 1000), collection="test"),
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("8", 1000), collection="test"),
            kv_store.aget_all(),
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("9", 1000)),
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("10", 1000)),
            get_all_keys_individually(kv_store),
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("11", 1000)),
            kv_store.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("12", 1000)),
            kv_store.aget_all(),
            kv_store.aget_all(),
            kv_store.aget(key="1_1"),
            kv_store_2.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("5", 1000), collection="test"),
            kv_store_2.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("6", 1000), collection="test"),
            kv_store_2.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("7", 1000), collection="test"),
            kv_store_2.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("8", 1000), collection="test"),
            kv_store_2.aget_all(),
            get_all_keys_individually(kv_store_2),
            kv_store_2.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("9", 1000)),
            kv_store_2.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("10", 1000)),
            kv_store_2.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("11", 1000)),
            kv_store_2.aput_all(generate_kv_pairs("12", 1000)),
        ]

        _ = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)

        all_keys = await kv_store.aget_all()

        assert len(all_keys) == 12000

        assert not any(isinstance(task, Exception) for task in tasks)

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_async_deadlock_parallel(self, kv_store: DuckDBKVStore):

        tasks = [
            self.test_async_deadlock(kv_store),
            self.test_async_deadlock(kv_store),
        ]

        _ = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)

        assert not any(isinstance(task, Exception) for task in tasks)
```

run-llama/llama_index#10252.body:
### Bug Description

After updating llama index from 0.8.50 to 0.9.35 my vector query broke. MetadataFilters that use strings containing numeric values throw error complaining that valueNumber does not match valueText

### Version

0.9.35

### Steps to Reproduce

All you have to do is have a numeric string value in a metadata filter against a valueText field and the error will occur.

Seems to be a result of https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index/blob/851399a303a47972fb62b9bb8880434842e23dc3/llama_index/vector_stores/weaviate.py#L78 but not sure why that line was added in the first place

```py
import weaviate
from llama_index.schema import TextNode
from llama_index.vector_stores.weaviate import WeaviateVectorStore
from llama_index.vector_stores.types import MetadataFilters, MetadataFilter, VectorStoreQuery

client = weaviate.Client(url="http://localhost:8080")
vector_store = WeaviateVectorStore(weaviate_client=client, index_name="TestIndex", text_key="text")

vector_store.add(
    [
        TextNode(
            text="test 1",
            metadata={"article_id": "aaff"},
            embedding=[0, 0, 1],
        ),
        TextNode(
            text="test 2",
            metadata={"article_id": "1234"},
            embedding=[0, 1, 0],
        ),
        TextNode(
            text="test 3",
            metadata={"article_id": "3ff3"},
            embedding=[1, 0, 0],
        )
    ]
)

# -- working query
query = VectorStoreQuery(
    query_embedding=[0, 0, 0],
    similarity_top_k=2,
    filters=MetadataFilters(
        filters=[
            MetadataFilter(key="article_id", value="3ff3") # NOTE: works fine because is not a number
        ]
    )
)
results = vector_store.query(query)
print(results)

# -- below query breaks
query = VectorStoreQuery(
    query_embedding=[0, 0, 0],
    similarity_top_k=2,
    filters=MetadataFilters(
        filters=[
            MetadataFilter(key="article_id", value="1234") # NOTE: breaks because it's a number but the metadata field is a text field
        ]
    )
)
results = vector_store.query(query)
print(results)
```

### Relevant Logs/Tracbacks

```shell
results = vector_store.query(query)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.10/site-packages/llama_index/vector_stores/weaviate.py", line 338, in query
    parsed_result = parse_get_response(query_result)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.10/site-packages/llama_index/vector_stores/weaviate_utils.py", line 65, in parse_get_response
    raise ValueError("Invalid query, got errors: {}".format(response["errors"]))
ValueError: Invalid query, got errors: [{'locations': [{'column': 6, 'line': 1}], 'message': 'invalid \'where\' filter: child operand at position 0: data type filter cannot use "valueNumber" on type "text", use "valueText" instead', 'path': ['Get', 'User91609c28065a470e96c78465a7f40d6dReport1']}]
```
run-llama/llama_index#10252.title: [Bug]: Weaviate MetadataFilters break on "numeric" strings

run-llama/llama_index#10682.body:
### Bug Description

I previously implemented a VectorIndexRetriever using LlamaIndex's built-in vector index without any vector database, with MMR mode and mmr_threshold. It worked fine.

I then added ChromaDB and found that MMR mode works as long as you don't include the mmr_threshold. If you set the mmr_threshold in the vector_store_kwargs argument, you get an error.

### Version

llama-index-0.10.1

### Steps to Reproduce

Here are the relevant parts of my code. If I comment out the line indicated by "# ERROR", then the code works.

```
from llama_index.core.indices.vector_store.base import VectorStoreIndex
from llama_index.core.storage import StorageContext
from llama_index.core.indices.vector_store.retrievers.retriever import (
    VectorIndexRetriever,
)
from llama_index.core.query_engine.retriever_query_engine import (
    RetrieverQueryEngine
)
from llama_index.core import Settings
from llama_index.vector_stores.chroma.base import ChromaVectorStore
import chromadb

def run_query(
    question: str,
    vectorstore: str,
    top_k: int,
    mmr_threshold: float,
) -> RetrieverQueryEngine | None:
    '''
    Return an LLM response to an input query after doing a vector search.

    Args:
        question (str): The query to the LLM.
        vectorstore (str): Folder name of vector database.
        top_k (int): Number of retrievals or citations to retrieve via
            vector search.
        mmr_threshold (float): A float between 0 and 1, for MMR search mode.
            Closer to 0 gives you more diversity in the retrievals.
            Closer to 1 gives you more relevance in the retrievals.

    Returns:
        RetrieverQueryEngine | None: If vectorstore location exists, return a
            Response object from RetrieverQueryEngine, else return nothing.
    '''

    if not os.path.exists(vectorstore):
        print('Error: Vectorstore', vectorstore, 'not found!')
        return
    else:
        # Instantiate a Chroma client, setting storage folder location:
        client = chromadb.PersistentClient(path=vectorstore)

        # Instantiate a Chroma collection based on the client:
        chroma_collection = client.get_or_create_collection(vectorstore)

        # Instantiate a ChromaVectorStore based on the Chroma collection:
        vector_store = ChromaVectorStore(chroma_collection=chroma_collection)

        # Instantiate a storage context based on the ChromaVectorStore:
        storage_context = StorageContext.from_defaults(
            vector_store=vector_store
        )

        # Instantiate LLM and embedding model:
        llm, embedding = create_azure_models()

        # Add these 2 models to the LlamaIndex Settings:
        Settings.llm = llm
        Settings.embed_model = embedding

        index = VectorStoreIndex.from_vector_store(
            vector_store,
            storage_context=storage_context
        )

        # Instantiate and configure a VectorIndexRetriever:
        # Note about parameters:
        # similarity_top_k sets the number of retrievals (citations).
        # mmr_threshold is a value between 0 and 1.
        # Closer to 0 gives you more diversity.
        # Closer to 1 gives you more relevance.
        # If the data contains duplicated entries, set it lower (e.g. 0.2)
        # so that the retriever will skip over search results that are
        # identical or very similar and go for greater diversity.
        retriever = VectorIndexRetriever(
            index=index,
            similarity_top_k=top_k,
            vector_store_query_mode='mmr',
            vector_store_kwargs={'mmr_threshold': mmr_threshold}  # ERROR
        )

        # Instantiate RetrieverQueryEngine and pass in the VectorIndexRetriever:
        query_engine = RetrieverQueryEngine(
            retriever=retriever
        )

        # Query the index:
        response = query_engine.query(question)

        return response

```

### Relevant Logs/Tracbacks

```shell
File ".../text_data_helpers.py", line 142, in run_query
    response = query_engine.query(question)
  File ".../lib/python3.10/site-packages/llama_index/core/base/base_query_engine.py", line 40, in query
    return self._query(str_or_query_bundle)
  File ".../lib/python3.10/site-packages/llama_index/core/query_engine/retriever_query_engine.py", line 186, in _query
    nodes = self.retrieve(query_bundle)
  File ".../lib/python3.10/site-packages/llama_index/core/query_engine/retriever_query_engine.py", line 142, in retrieve
    nodes = self._retriever.retrieve(query_bundle)
  File ".../lib/python3.10/site-packages/llama_index/core/base/base_retriever.py", line 229, in retrieve
    nodes = self._retrieve(query_bundle)
  File ".../lib/python3.10/site-packages/llama_index/core/indices/vector_store/retrievers/retriever.py", line 94, in _retrieve
    return self._get_nodes_with_embeddings(query_bundle)
  File ".../lib/python3.10/site-packages/llama_index/core/indices/vector_store/retrievers/retriever.py", line 170, in _get_nodes_with_embeddings
    query_result = self._vector_store.query(query, **self._kwargs)
  File ".../lib/python3.10/site-packages/llama_index/vector_stores/chroma/base.py", line 292, in query
    results = self._collection.query(
TypeError: Collection.query() got an unexpected keyword argument 'mmr_threshold'
```

run-llama/llama_index#12209.body:
### Feature Description

The implementation of ReAct Agent today has a feature of limiting the number of steps it can take to serve one request. This is nice, but it is carried out in a brutal way of raising a ValueError that isn't handled gracefully (read: on the semantic level) in upper layers of the framework:

```
  File "...core/agent/react/step.py", line 646, in run_step
    return self._run_step(step, task)
  File "...llama_index/core/agent/react/step.py", line 462, in _run_step
    agent_response = self._get_response(
  File "...llama_index/core/agent/react/step.py", line 334, in _get_response
    raise ValueError("Reached max iterations.")
ValueError: Reached max iterations.
```

You can't blame the Agent when such exceptions happen and halts your whole program. The Agent didn't even know that such limit existed!

To handle this more gracefully, we can either ask all developers to add "finish your chain-of-thought in at most 10 steps", or we can do this at the implementation level:

- Option 1: Append a "you have X tries remaining" to each `ObservationReasoningStep` as the tool output, prompting the Agent to make careful use of the remaining allowances. (Of course, make this string overridable, so that users can localize it to their own language.)
- Option 2: At `len(current_reasoning) == self._max_iterations`, instead of raising the `ValueError` blindly, return the response appended with "you have no more tries left" then force set `is_done` to `True`.

### Reason

IMO, it's a waste of the versatility of LLMs to leave exceptions unhandled. Along the same line of thought as in https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index/pull/12207 , I think we should pipe exceptions back to LLMs as much as we can across the whole framework.

Here's my philosophy: We can't just treat the LLM as a infant who we want to shield from all the real world's harms; we should start trusting it to handle its own failures and give it a chance to correct itself.

### Value of Feature

This would make LlamaIndex look less fragile to developers.
run-llama/llama_index#12209.title: [Feature Request]: ReAct Agent: Inform the Agent that it's running out of steps

run-llama/llama_index#12699.body:
### Question Validation

- [X] I have searched both the documentation and discord for an answer.

### Question

The question I asked it is "What's today's date"

I already created a simple function tool to answer this correctly to eliminate hallucination:
```
    def current_date(self) -> str:
        """
        Get current date
        
        args:
            None
        """
        return datetime.today().strftime('%B %d, %Y')
```
This is the output when I call `agent.chat(...)`:
```
  Observation: Error: Could not parse output. Please follow the thought-action-input format. Try again.
  Thought: The current language of the user is: English. I need to use a tool to help me answer the question.
  Action: current_date
  Action Input: {'type': 'object', 'properties': AttributedDict()}
  Observation: Error: current_date() got an unexpected keyword argument 'type'
  Thought: The current language of the user is: English. I need to use a tool to help me answer the question.
  Action: current_date
  Action Input: {'type': 'object'}
  Observation: Error: current_date() got an unexpected keyword argument 'type'
  Thought: The current language of the user is: English. I need to use a tool to help me answer the question.
  Action: current_date
  Action Input: {}
  Observation: April 10, 2024
  Thought: Now that we know the current date, let's find out what day of the week it is.
  Action: current_date
  Action Input: {'type': 'object'}
  Observation: Error: current_date() got an unexpected keyword argument 'type'
  Thought: The current language of the user is: English. I need to use a tool to help me answer the question.
  Action: current_date
  Action Input: {}
  Observation: April 10, 2024
  Thought: Now that we know the current date, let's find out what day of the week it is.
  Action: query_tool
  Action Input: {'input': "What day is 'April 10, 2024'?"}
```

As you can see, sometimes it gets it, and sometimes it get's this:
```
  Action Input: {'type': 'object'}
  Observation: Error: current_date() got an unexpected keyword argument 'type'
```
it's always unstable, and sometimes it just hallucinates till it chooses the wrong tool and other times it get's to max iterations after running for a while.

Is there something I'm missing, or is this just a bug, also how can I fix it.
run-llama/llama_index#12699.title: [Question]: I keep having issues when a react agent tries to use a tool

run-llama/llama_index#13182.body:
### Feature Description

At a high level, there are two separate features that are worth considering:
1. Use Solr as a document retriever which can then be integrated with an LLM-based response synthesizer.
2. Use Solr as a vector and document store. Offload vector search to Solr to retrieve relevant documents.

### Reason

LlamaIndex does not know how to efficiently query a Solr cluster to retrieve results.

### Value of Feature

Document Indexing pipelines target ElasticSearch and Solr.
ElasticSearch integration is already existent, and Solr still has a wide user base, so this is a natural extension.
run-llama/llama_index#13182.title: [Feature Request]: Add Support for Apache Solr

run-llama/llama_index#14340.body:
### Feature Description

Openai_like LLMs are supported, however, it would be useful to do this with embedding models. This would allow users to integrate custom frameworks more easily. Namely, deepspeed fastgen model inference or vLLM, as currently I can't see an easy way to integrate local (or hosted) pipeline parallelised embedding models.

### Reason

There is no way to integrate a custom API endpoint with embedding models and only recognised frameworks are supported

### Value of Feature

This will increase compatibility and enable embedding models to be parallelised across GPUs with frameworks such as DeepSpeed-mii and vLLM (I assume).
run-llama/llama_index#14340.title: [Feature Request]: Openai_like embedding model integration

run-llama/llama_index#14401.body:
### Bug Description

The use of the "model" instead of "model_name" in BedrockEmbedding class led to the automatic selection of the default model and doesn't raise an error. This might lead to use the wrong model.

Тhe following code calls the default model which is "amazon.titan-embed-text-v1" :
model = BedrockEmbedding(**model** = "cohere.embed-multilingual-v3")

The following code calls the desired model ("cohere.embed-multilingual-v3"):
model = BedrockEmbedding(**model_name** = "cohere.embed-multilingual-v3")

### Version

0.10.44

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Import the BedrockEmbedding class from the llama_index package in the Python script or notebook

2. Instantiate the BedrockEmbedding class using the **model** instead of **model_name**. This is the incorrect usage that leads to the automatic selection of the default model.
model = BedrockEmbedding(model="cohere.embed-multilingual-v3")

3. Since the model parameter is incorrectly used, the BedrockEmbedding class should automatically select the default model ("amazon.titan-embed-text-v1") instead of the desired model ("cohere.embed-multilingual-v3"). There should be no error message indicating that the wrong parameter was used, which is the crux of the bug.

4. Attempt to instantiate the BedrockEmbedding class using the correct parameter, model_name, to specify the desired model.
model = BedrockEmbedding(model_name="cohere.embed-multilingual-v3")

### Relevant Logs/Tracbacks

_No response_
run-llama/llama_index#14401.title: [Bug]: model_name vs. model in BedrockEmbedding doesn't throw an error

run-llama/llama_index#14415.body:
### Bug Description

The FunctionCallingProgram class takes in a tool_choice parameter but does not use it.

The use varies slightly for OpenAI and Anthropic models:
https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/function-calling
https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/tool-use

### Version

0.10.37

### Steps to Reproduce

```from pydantic import Field

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from llama_index.core.program import FunctionCallingProgram
from llama_index.llms.anthropic import Anthropic
from pydantic import BaseModel

model = Anthropic("claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620")
    
class TestResponse(BaseModel):
    message: str = Field(description="The response message")

prompt = "Generate a song about {topic}. DO NOT USE ANY TOOLS"
program = FunctionCallingProgram.from_defaults(
    output_cls=TestResponse,
    prompt_template_str=prompt,
    verbose=True,
    llm=model,
    tool_choice={"type": "any"}
)
output = program(topic="dogs")

### Relevant Logs/Tracbacks

```shell
File ~/qqqqq/.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/llama_index/llms/anthropic/base.py:411, in Anthropic.get_tool_calls_from_response(self, response, error_on_no_tool_call, **kwargs)
    409 if len(tool_calls) < 1:
    410     if error_on_no_tool_call:
--> 411         raise ValueError(
    412             f"Expected at least one tool call, but got {len(tool_calls)} tool calls."
    413         )
    414     else:
    415         return []

ValueError: Expected at least one tool call, but got 0 tool calls.
```
run-llama/llama_index#14415.title: [Bug]: FunctionCallingProgram ignores tool_choice parameter

run-llama/llama_index#14435.body:
### Question Validation

- [x] I have searched both the documentation and discord for an answer.

### Question

I don't know what I am doing wrong. I chunked a few hundred documents using the HierarchicalNodeParser and stored them in a lanceDB database using [this guide](https://docs.llamaindex.ai/en/stable/examples/vector_stores/LanceDBIndexDemo/). It has about 24000 leaf nodes in it.

If I want to query the data, I use the code down below:

```
embed_model = OpenAIEmbedding(model="text-embedding-3-large")

db = lancedb.connect("./lancedb_RL_test_2")
Settings.embed_model = embed_model
table = db.open_table("docs_OpenAI")
vector_store = LanceDBVectorStore.from_table(table)
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_vector_store(vector_store)
storage_context = StorageContext.from_defaults(persist_dir="./RL_test_2")
base_retriever = index.as_retriever(similarity_top_k=10000)

question = "test"
nodes = base_retriever.retrieve(question)
```

What this seems to be doing is initially grabbing the same exact 1080 nodes from the database, then ranking them based on vector similarity to query. I tried tuning the overfetch_factor and nprobes parameters of the LanceDBVectorStore, but this seems to do nothing. I am very confused on what I might be doing wrong? Any help?
run-llama/llama_index#14435.title: [Question]: Index created from a LanceDBVectorStore is not returning all nodes?

run-llama/llama_index#14940.body:
### Bug Description

I might be looking at it wrong, but the async functions in [VectorStoreIndex](https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index/blob/3be95a28e2bda8f13ceb0a2a0622bb4f7cf6fb14/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/indices/vector_store/base.py#L295) only seem to get triggered by `build_index_from_nodes` function and nothing else. 

For example:

- if we use `insert_nodes` it calls --> `self._insert(nodes, **insert_kwargs)` 
- `_insert` only calls `self._add_nodes_to_index(self._index_struct, nodes, **insert_kwargs)`
- this completely circumvents the `_async_add_nodes_to_index` 

In fact the only usage of the `use_async` parameter is in the `build_index_from_nodes`

Is this the intended behavior? Seems unlikely... 

### Version

v0.10.57

### Steps to Reproduce

Just try to use async for anything BUT the `build_index_from_nodes` function.

### Relevant Logs/Tracbacks

```shell
NA
```
run-llama/llama_index#14940.title: [Bug]: VectorStoreIndex insert async not working

run-llama/llama_index#15529.body:
### Bug Description

I am creating and saving an VectorStoreIndex in a similar way it is described in the [starter example](https://docs.llamaindex.ai/en/stable/getting_started/starter_example/). The main goal is to read all files in the specified directory ("PERSIST_DIR") and add them to the index: 
```python
documents = SimpleDirectoryReader(directory, filename_as_id=True).load_data()
try:
    # Load index from storage
    print("Loading index from storage...")
    storage_context = StorageContext.from_defaults(persist_dir=PERSIST_DIR)
    index = load_index_from_storage(storage_context)
except FileNotFoundError:
    # Create index from documents and persist it
    print("Creating index from documents...")
    index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(documents)
    index.storage_context.persist(persist_dir=PERSIST_DIR)
```
If a file is deleted from the folder, it should also be deleted from the index. To do this, the following code should compare the content of the index with the content of the folder and delete the files missing in the folder from the index. 
```python
print("Deleting missing documents...")
for id, doc in index.ref_doc_info.items():
    if not os.path.exists(doc.metadata['file_path']):
        print(f"Deleting missing document: {doc.metadata['file_path']}")
        index.delete_ref_doc(
            doc.metadata['file_name'], delete_from_docstore=True)
index.storage_context.persist(persist_dir=PERSIST_DIR)
```

The problem is: The index.delete_ref_doc-call does exactly nothing and the query is performed with the "deleted" document. This can be seen in the attatched logs, between "Deleting missing documents..." and "Performing query..."

Feel free to ask if any further information is needed!

### Version

0.10.65

- Add files to the index
- persist the index
- remove one file from the index

run-llama/llama_index#15880.body:
### Feature Description

Currently, when `OpensearchVectorClient` class is being created, it checks for the requested index to exist, and creates it, if it does not.

It is implemented [here](https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index/blob/main/llama-index-integrations/vector_stores/llama-index-vector-stores-opensearch/llama_index/vector_stores/opensearch/base.py#L111) and implicitly runs an asynchronous event loop.

There need to be a way to run in synchronously.

### Reason

It all works fine until you decide to run it in some fancy environment like **Jupyter Notebook**, that uses event loop for its own needs. ;-)

Code example:
```python
os_client=OpensearchVectorClient(endpoint=os_endpoint,
                                 index='documents_test',
                                 dim=config.OS_EMBEDDING_MODEL_DIM,
                                 embedding_field='article.paragraphs.content_embedding',
                                 text_field='article.paragraphs.content',
                                 method={
                                    "name": "hnsw",
                                    "space_type": "innerproduct",
                                    "engine": "nmslib"
                                 })
```
Error returned:
```
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
RuntimeError                              Traceback (most recent call last)
Cell In[6], line 1
----> 1 os_client=OpensearchVectorClient(endpoint=os_endpoint,
      2                                  index='documents_test',
      3                                  dim=config.OS_EMBEDDING_MODEL_DIM,
      4                                  embedding_field='article.paragraphs.content_embedding',
      5                                  text_field='article.paragraphs.content',
      6                                  method={
      7                                     "name": "hnsw",
      8                                     "space_type": "innerproduct",
      9                                     "engine": "nmslib"
     10                                  })

File /usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/llama_index/vector_stores/opensearch/base.py:118, in OpensearchVectorClient.__init__(self, endpoint, index, dim, embedding_field, text_field, method, engine, max_chunk_bytes, search_pipeline, os_client, **kwargs)
    116 event_loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
    117 try:
--> 118     event_loop.run_until_complete(
    119         self._os_client.indices.get(index=self._index)
    120     )
    121 except not_found_error:
    122     event_loop.run_until_complete(
    123         self._os_client.indices.create(index=self._index, body=idx_conf)
    124     )

File /usr/local/lib/python3.12/asyncio/base_events.py:663, in BaseEventLoop.run_until_complete(self, future)
    652 """Run until the Future is done.
    653 
    654 If the argument is a coroutine, it is wrapped in a Task.
   (...)
    660 Return the Future's result, or raise its exception.
    661 """
    662 self._check_closed()
--> 663 self._check_running()
    665 new_task = not futures.isfuture(future)
    666 future = tasks.ensure_future(future, loop=self)

File /usr/local/lib/python3.12/asyncio/base_events.py:622, in BaseEventLoop._check_running(self)
    620 def _check_running(self):
    621     if self.is_running():
--> 622         raise RuntimeError('This event loop is already running')
    623     if events._get_running_loop() is not None:
    624         raise RuntimeError(
    625             'Cannot run the event loop while another loop is running')

RuntimeError: This event loop is already running
```

### Value of Feature

Being able to work with llama-index from a Jupyter Notebook.
run-llama/llama_index#15880.title: [Feature Request]: OpensearchVectorClient should allow synchronous initialization

### Bug Description

When using Structured Output, it is expected to return a Pydantic Model: https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index/blob/main/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/llms/llm.py#L323
However, there are some cases that response from LLM can't be parsed into a Pydantic Model, and this [line](https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index/blob/main/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/llms/structured_llm.py#L80) will trigger an AttributeError. 

Maybe we can implement a retry and re-generate when the output raise validation error from Pydantic?

### Version

0.11.17

### Steps to Reproduce

```
from typing import List, Tuple

from llama_index.core.bridge.pydantic import BaseModel
from llama_index.core.llms import LLM
from llama_index.core.prompts import ChatMessage


class QuestionAndAnswerList(BaseModel):
    questions: List[str]
    answers: List[str]


if __name__ == '__main__':
    sllm = llm.as_structured_llm(output_cls=QuestionAndAnswerList)
    response = sllm.chat(
        [system_prompt, user_prompt]
    )
```

```

run-llama/llama_index#17324.body:
### Bug Description

When creating a FunctionTool and using the Pydantic Field object to specify the "default" value, the default value is not set when calling the function. Somehow the type of the argument is `pydantic.fields.FieldInfo`

### Version

lama-index                             0.12.5

### Steps to Reproduce

```
from pydantic import Field
from llama_index.core.tools import FunctionTool
from typing import Optional


def get_weather(
    location: Optional[str] = Field(
        default="Berlin"
    ),
) -> str:
    """Usfeful for getting the weather for a given location."""
    if location == "Berlin":
        return "nice weather in Berlin"
    return "bad weather!"


tool = FunctionTool.from_defaults(get_weather)
```

```
> tool().content
'bad weather!'

> tool("Berlin").content
'nice weather in Berlin'
```

### Relevant Logs/Tracbacks

This fixes it by the way:

```
from pydantic import Field
from pydantic.fields import FieldInfo
from llama_index.core.tools import FunctionTool
from typing import Optional


def get_weather(
    location: Optional[str] = Field(
        default="Berlin"
    ),
) -> str:
    """Usfeful for getting the weather for a given location."""
    if isinstance(location, FieldInfo):
        location = location.get_default()
    if location == "Berlin":
        return "nice weather in Berlin"
    return "bad weather!"


tool = FunctionTool.from_defaults(get_weather)
```
run-llama/llama_index#17324.title: [Bug]: "default" argument in Field not respected for FunctionTool

### Bug Description

I create a ChatMessage with one text and multiple image blocks but only the text block is provided to Gemini.

The problem is during the message conversion:
https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index/blob/v0.12.19/llama-index-integrations/llms/llama-index-llms-vertex/llama_index/llms/vertex/base.py#L228

Here the message content is used but that is a legacy solution that returns only the text content of the message.
The blocks field should be used here:

https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index/blob/v0.12.19/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py#L177

In the _convert_gemini_part_to_prompt method there is code to handle images but as far as I can tell this method is only called with string input so that code is never executed.

Create a Vertex LLM.
Call the chat method on the llm with the chat message and check what is provided for the LLM.

run-llama/llama_index#17961.body:
### Feature Description

The existing `allow_parallel_tool_calls` option isn't sent to OpenAI.  It is used to filter the output in some flows though.

Workaround: 
Pass `parallel_tool_calls=False` via llm_kwargs or similar.

### Reason

The lack of this feature can cause issues when expecting a single response (e.g. as_structured_llm).  The LLM can split it's response across multiple function calls, and then the framework drops all but the first.

It's made a bit worse since it looks like the framework supports disabling parallel calls via `allow_parallel_tool_calls`, and that does not currently work.

### Value of Feature

_No response_
run-llama/llama_index#17961.title: [Feature Request]: OpenAI - connect allow_parallel_tool_calls to parallel_tool_calls option

- [x] I have searched both the documentation and discord for an answer.

### Question

Searching and debugging a long time to find a solution. Thanks for any help!

When an agent workflow is prompted to execute multiple tools that contain human in the loop events at once,  the workflow get stuck. 

-For example asking my agent to create 5 'interview questions' results in  attempting to calling the following tool 5 times, as expected. 
- The tool seems to be called in parallel, 5 back to back executions. If context is `set` during these executions, other tools are not aware of it, as it happens at nearly the same time.
-  When the agent reaches the line of code to await a human response event, further executions of the tool call are lost. The agent sometimes only can call the tool 3/5 times before the function awaits the human in the loop response. The context gets serialized and saved... resulting in a loss of tool calls.
- It seems I need to either prevent this tool from being called in parallel, or figure out how to wait for all planned tool calls to execute before awaiting the human response event. 

Thanks again for any help! 

```
# Create Question Tool
async def create_interview_question(
    ctx: Context, current_user: User, interview_uuid: str, question: str
):
    # Start Tool
    # ... logic to check if is_new_question
   
    try:
        if is_new_question:
            ctx.write_event_to_stream(
                InputRequiredEvent(
                    prefix="Confirm you want to add question, respond "yes"."
                )
            )

        event = await ctx.wait_for_event(HumanResponseEvent)

        if event.response.lower() == "yes":
            create_question(...)

            if not interview_question:
                raise Exception("Failed to create interview question.")

            return "The question has been created. Proceed to make the next question. When all questions are created, inform the user!"
        else:
            return f"The user has provided additional input: {event.response}"

    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(e)
        raise e
```

### Bug Description

Title says everything.

In section *Steps to Reproduce* I will give 2 code snippets with the same workflow, but steps are renamed. In the first case, everything works fine, but second case doesn't halt.

### Version

0.12.25

### Steps to Reproduce

I'm sorry, but I spent a lot of time finding this bug in my own `Workflow`. I then extracted it to a "sandbox" file and tried to make it as minimal as possible.

1) First case of naming - works:

Diagram:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1c753800-0791-4095-ab03-f0c6086fe8af)

Yes, I really tried hard to minimize it.

```python
import asyncio
from typing import Optional
from llama_index.core.workflow import Context, Event, StartEvent, StopEvent, Workflow, draw_all_possible_flows, step


class Initial1(Event): ...
class Initial2(Event): ...

class Intermediate3(Event): ...
class Intermediate1(Event): ...
class Intermediate2(Event): ...
class Intermediate4(Event): ...


class TestWorkflow(Workflow):
    @step
    async def start(self, ctx: Context, ev: StartEvent) -> Initial1 | Initial2 | None:
        ctx.send_event(Initial1())
        ctx.send_event(Initial2())

    @step
    async def make_intermediate_1(self, ctx: Context, evs: Initial1) -> Optional[Intermediate1]:
        return Intermediate1()

    @step
    async def make_intermediate_2(self, ctx: Context, evs: Initial1 | Intermediate1) -> Optional[Intermediate2]:
        got = ctx.collect_events(evs, [Initial1, Intermediate1])
       
        if not got:
            return None

        return Intermediate2()

    @step
    async def make_intermediate_3(self, ctx: Context, evs: Initial2) -> Optional[Intermediate3]:
        return Intermediate3()

    @step
    async def make_intermediate_4(self, ctx: Context, evs: Initial1 | Intermediate1 | Intermediate3) -> Optional[Intermediate4]:
        got = ctx.collect_events(evs, [Initial1, Intermediate1, Intermediate3])
        
        if not got:
            return None
        
        return Intermediate4()

    @step
    async def finish(self, ctx: Context, evs: Intermediate1 | Intermediate2 | Intermediate3 | Intermediate4) -> Optional[StopEvent]:
        got = ctx.collect_events(evs, [Intermediate1, Intermediate2, Intermediate3, Intermediate4])

        if not got:
            return None

        return StopEvent()


async def main():
    w = TestWorkflow(verbose=True)
    draw_all_possible_flows(w, filename="test.html")
    await w.run()
    
    
asyncio.run(main())
```

run-llama/llama_index#18384.body:
### Feature Description

### Request to update text-generation dependencies for llama-index-llms-huggingface v0.4.2

**Security concern**

The pyproject.toml currently pins:
```
text-generation = "^0.7.0"
```
However, this package version is impacted by [CVE-2024-3924](https://github.com/advisories/[REDACTED_GHSA]), which describes a code injection vulnerability in the huggingface/text-generation-inference repository.

A GitHub Actions workflow uses unsanitized github.head_ref input, potentially allowing attackers to execute arbitrary code during PR workflows.

### Reason

_No response_

### Value of Feature

While this CVE targets the GitHub Actions CI of the text-generation-inference project, environments using this package may prefer to update to the patched version (2.0.0) or later for compliance and peace of mind.

It will benefit users who are developing projects or tools under strict security environments.
run-llama/llama_index#18384.title: [Feature Request]: Security concern CVE-2024-3924 - To update llama-index-llms-huggingface dependencies text-generation to >= v2.0.0

run-llama/llama_index#18388.body:
### Feature Description

Currently, the HuggingFaceEmbedding class, which use the SentenceTransformers library, does not support passing the show_progress_bar=False parameter to disable the progress bar during encoding.

This feature is available in the [SentenceTransformers' encode function](https://github.com/UKPLab/sentence-transformers/blob/8d73d4f0fceee03ce68df1c8e7f0c0fe843c4988/sentence_transformers/SentenceTransformer.py#L402C9-L402C32), allowing users to suppress progress bars when encoding large datasets for cleaner logs and better performance in headless environments.

However, the current implementation of the [HuggingFaceEmbedding function](https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index/blob/e78fd4e86f38c95fc5e94ed4c4e3da848e5a99bd/llama-index-integrations/embeddings/llama-index-embeddings-huggingface/llama_index/embeddings/huggingface/base.py#L219) lacks support for passing additional keyword arguments (kwargs), which prevents the show_progress_bar option from being utilized.

### Reason

- Small Feature Request
- Cleaner logs without progress bars

### Value of Feature

Stop flooding the log
run-llama/llama_index#18388.title: [Feature Request]: Remove progress bar in HuggingFaceEmbedding

run-llama/llama_index#18400.body:
### Feature Description

IBM watsonx Python SDK added the `persistent_connection` flag in 1.1.2 and enabled it by default. This causes problems with certain environments where network connections are cut from time to time, such as OpenShift/Kubernetes. There should be a way to disable it in llama_index.

### Reason

It is not possible to disable the persistent connection via llama_index today.

### Value of Feature

_No response_
run-llama/llama_index#18400.title: [Feature Request]: IBM WatsonxLLM doesn't support `persistent_connection` configurable

run-llama/llama_index#18406.body:
## Issue Description
In the OpenAIResponses class, the `built_in_tools` instance attribute is being directly assigned to the `tools` key in model kwargs without being copied. This leads to unintended modifications of the original `built_in_tools` list when the tools list is modified later in the code.

## Location
File: https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index/blob/main/llama-index-integrations/llms/llama-index-llms-openai/llama_index/llms/openai/responses.py#L312

Class: `OpenAIResponses`
Method: `_get_model_kwargs`

## Current Behavior
```python
model_kwargs = {
    # ...
    "tools": self.built_in_tools,  # Direct assignment without copy
    # ...
}

# Later in the code
if "tools" in kwargs:
    if isinstance(model_kwargs["tools"], list):
        model_kwargs["tools"].extend(kwargs.pop("tools"))  # This modifies self.built_in_tools
```

## Expected Behavior
The `built_in_tools` list should be copied before being assigned to model_kwargs to prevent unintended modifications:

```python
model_kwargs = {
    # ...
    "tools": self.built_in_tools.copy() if self.built_in_tools else None,
    # ...
}
```


## Impact
This bug can cause:
1. Unintended side effects where built_in_tools grows with each call
2. Inconsistent behavior in subsequent API calls

## Suggested Fix
Replace the direct assignment with a copy operation:
```python
def _get_model_kwargs(self, **kwargs: Any) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    model_kwargs = {
        "model": self.model,
        "include": self.include,
        "instructions": self.instructions,
        "max_output_tokens": self.max_output_tokens,
        "metadata": self.call_metadata,
        "previous_response_id": self._previous_response_id,
        "store": self.store,
        "temperature": self.temperature,
        "tools": self.built_in_tools.copy() if self.built_in_tools else None,  # Create a copy
        "top_p": self.top_p,
        "truncation": self.truncation,
        "user": self.user,
    }
    # ... rest of the method
```

## Additional Notes
- This issue affects all methods that use `_get_model_kwargs`, including chat, completion, and streaming endpoints
- The bug becomes more apparent when making multiple calls with different tool configurations
- Consider adding a test case to verify that `built_in_tools` remains unchanged after API calls

## Environment
- LlamaIndex version: [version]
- Python version: 3.10
- OpenAI API version: [version]

### Version

latest

### Steps to Reproduce

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Create an OpenAIResponses instance with built_in_tools
2. Call any method that uses _get_model_kwargs with additional tools in kwargs
3. Check self.built_in_tools - it will have been modified with the additional tools

### Relevant Logs/Tracbacks

```shell

```
run-llama/llama_index#18406.title: [Bug]: Unintended Modification of built_in_tools in OpenAIResponses

run-llama/llama_index#18416.body:
### Question Validation

- [x] I have searched both the documentation and discord for an answer.

### Question

In the `AgentWorkflow`, I noticed that the **state** is being appended to the **chat history** in each iteration. This behavior results in the **current state** being appended to a chat history entry that already contains the **previous state**, leading to compounding and redundant entries.

#### Steps Observed
1. In the first iteration, the **chat history** is updated by appending the **current state**. This behavior occurs in [multi_agent_workflow.py#L355-L360](https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index/blob/b79bf64d9a8eb79a00df950172781a65073521a6/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/agent/workflow/multi_agent_workflow.py#L355-L360), creating a new chat history entry that includes both the original user message and the current state.
2. In subsequent iterations, the **chat history** entry from the previous iteration (which already contains the previous state) is updated again by appending the **current state**. This results in the **current state** being appended to a chat history entry that already contains the **previous state**, leading to duplication.

#### Example
**Initial State**:
```json
{
    "research_note": {},
    "report_content": "Not written yet",
    "review": "Review required"
}
```

**First Iteration**:
- Chat History Entry: `"What are the key findings from the research?"`
- Updated Entry:  
  ```
  Shared Agent State:
  {"research_note": {}, "report_content": "Not written yet", "review": "Review required"}

  Current User Message:
  What are the key findings from the research?
  ```

**Second Iteration**:
- Instead of replacing the state, the workflow appends the new state:
  ```
  Shared Agent State:
  {"research_note": {"summary": "Key findings summarized"}, "report_content": "Not written yet", "review": "Review required"}

  Current User Message:
  Shared Agent State:
  {"research_note": {}, "report_content": "Not written yet", "review": "Review required"}

  Current User Message:
  What are the key findings from the research?
  ```

#### Question
Is this behavior of appending the **current state** to the **chat history** in each iteration intended?  If not, would it make sense to update the **state object** in the chat history with the **current state** instead of appending it?

#### Environment
- ** Version**: `llama-index 0.12.29`
run-llama/llama_index#18416.title: [Question]: Is State Duplication in Chat History Across Workflow Iterations Intended?

run-llama/llama_index#18443.body:
### Bug Description

When using `OpensearchVectorClient` for Opensearch Serverless (AWS) as Vector Store. The implementation checks if the index exists or not first, if not, it creates an index and checks again by using `.refresh()` method, but that is not supported by Opensearch Serverless, and it returns an error 404 index not found.

Implementation:
```python
try:
            self._os_client.indices.get(index=self._index)
        except TypeError:
            # Probably using async so switch to async client
            try:
                asyncio_run(self._os_async_client.indices.get(index=self._index))
            except not_found_error:
                asyncio_run(
                    self._os_async_client.indices.create(
                        index=self._index, body=idx_conf
                    )
                )
                asyncio_run(self._os_async_client.indices.refresh(index=self._index))
        except not_found_error:
            self._os_client.indices.create(index=self._index, body=idx_conf)
            self._os_client.indices.refresh(index=self._index)
```

**References:** https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchainjs/issues/2302 

### Version

0.12.14

### Steps to Reproduce

Simply attempt to use the implementation by passing an index name that is not present in your Opensearch Serverless collection.

### Relevant Logs/Tracbacks

```shell

```
run-llama/llama_index#18443.title: [Bug]: 404 - Index not found Error when using AWS Opensearch Serverless

run-llama/llama_index#18465.body:
### Bug Description

`llama-index-callbacks-arize-phoenix` [requires](https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index/blob/main/llama-index-integrations/callbacks/llama-index-callbacks-arize-phoenix/pyproject.toml#L34) Python `>=3.9,<3.13` which excludes the current stable version of Python. This prevents it from resolving in environments with strict Python version checking.

### Version

0.12.30

### Steps to Reproduce

(with uv)

```
$ uv init
$ uv python pin 3.13
$ uv add llama-index-callbacks-arize-phoenix
$ source .venv/bin/active
$ uv pip install -r pyproject.toml
$ uv pip check
Checked 64 packages in 1ms
Found 1 incompatibility
The package `llama-index-callbacks-arize-phoenix` requires Python >=3.9, <3.13, but `3.13.3` is installed
```
run-llama/llama_index#18465.title: [Bug]: llama-index-callbacks-arize-phoenix 0.5.0 requires Python <3.13

run-llama/llama_index#18476.body:
### Feature Description

The SentenceTransformerReRank abstracts away the Cross Encoder from sentence-transformers in a way the limits use such as inability to change cache directory or to use offline mode only. 

The CrossEncoder parameters should be enabled for SentenceTransformer

```

class CrossEncoder(PushToHubMixin):
    """
    A CrossEncoder takes exactly two sentences / texts as input and either predicts
    a score or label for this sentence pair. It can for example predict the similarity of the sentence pair
    on a scale of 0 ... 1.

    It does not yield a sentence embedding and does not work for individual sentences.

    Args:
        model_name (str): A model name from Hugging Face Hub that can be loaded with AutoModel, or a path to a local
            model. We provide several pre-trained CrossEncoder models that can be used for common tasks.
        num_labels (int, optional): Number of labels of the classifier. If 1, the CrossEncoder is a regression model that
            outputs a continuous score 0...1. If > 1, it output several scores that can be soft-maxed to get
            probability scores for the different classes. Defaults to None.
        max_length (int, optional): Max length for input sequences. Longer sequences will be truncated. If None, max
            length of the model will be used. Defaults to None.
        device (str, optional): Device that should be used for the model. If None, it will use CUDA if available.
            Defaults to None.
        automodel_args (Dict, optional): Arguments passed to AutoModelForSequenceClassification. Defaults to None.
        tokenizer_args (Dict, optional): Arguments passed to AutoTokenizer. Defaults to None.
        config_args (Dict, optional): Arguments passed to AutoConfig. Defaults to None.
        cache_dir (`str`, `Path`, optional): Path to the folder where cached files are stored.
        trust_remote_code (bool, optional): Whether or not to allow for custom models defined on the Hub in their own modeling files.
            This option should only be set to True for repositories you trust and in which you have read the code, as it
            will execute code present on the Hub on your local machine. Defaults to False.
        revision (Optional[str], optional): The specific model version to use. It can be a branch name, a tag name, or a commit id,
            for a stored model on Hugging Face. Defaults to None.
        local_files_only (bool, optional): If `True`, avoid downloading the model. Defaults to False.
        default_activation_function (Callable, optional): Callable (like nn.Sigmoid) about the default activation function that
            should be used on-top of model.predict(). If None. nn.Sigmoid() will be used if num_labels=1,
            else nn.Identity(). Defaults to None.
        classifier_dropout (float, optional): The dropout ratio for the classification head. Defaults to None.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        model_name: str,
        num_labels: int = None,
        max_length: int = None,
        device: str | None = None,
        automodel_args: dict = None,
        tokenizer_args: dict = None,
        config_args: dict = None,
        cache_dir: str = None,
        trust_remote_code: bool = False,
        revision: str | None = None,
        local_files_only: bool = False,
        default_activation_function=None,
        classifier_dropout: float = None,
```


### Reason

Enable fully features of CrossEncoder

### Value of Feature

Full use of CrossEncoder while still able to integrate other types through llamaindex
run-llama/llama_index#18476.title: [Feature Request]: Enable All CrossEncoder Settings

run-llama/llama_index#18478.body:
### Feature Description

LlamaIndex should support the new capability of GPT-4.1 that allows images to be included in assistant messages. Currently, when using ChatMessage with role="assistant" containing ImageBlock elements, these images are ignored when passed to the OpenAI API, despite GPT-4.1 supporting this functionality.

This currently doesn't work with LlamaIndex but is supported by GPT-4.1 API
chat_history = [
    ChatMessage(
        role="user",
        blocks=[TextBlock(text="What's the storage temperature range of the device?")]
    ),
    ChatMessage(
        role="assistant",
        blocks=[
            TextBlock(text="The storage temperature range is from -40°C to +70°C."),
            ImageBlock(path="./temperature_image.jpg")  # This image is ignored
        ]
    )
]

Current Behavior
When using LlamaIndex with GPT-4.1, ImageBlock elements in assistant messages are ignored and not passed to the OpenAI API. This is despite direct testing confirming that the OpenAI API for GPT-4.1 (but not GPT-4o or GPT-4o-mini) supports images in assistant messages.

Expected Behavior
LlamaIndex should detect when GPT-4.1 is being used and properly convert ImageBlock elements in assistant messages to the appropriate OpenAI API format:

{
    "role": "assistant",
    "content": [
        {"type": "text", "text": "..."},
        {
            "type": "image_url",
            "image_url": {"url": "data:image/jpeg;base64,..."}
        }
    ]
}


Reproduction Code
import base64
from openai import OpenAI

Direct API call works with GPT-4.1
client = OpenAI()
base64_image = encode_image("temperature_image.jpg")

This works with GPT-4.1 but not with GPT-4o/GPT-4o-mini
completion = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4.1",
    messages=[
        {"role": "user", "content": "What's the storage temperature range?"},
        {"role": "assistant", 
         "content": [
             {"type": "text", "text": "The range is -40°C to +70°C."},
             {"type": "image_url", "image_url": {"url": f"data:image/jpeg;base64,{base64_image}"}}
         ]
        },
        {"role": "user", "content": "What does the image show?"}
    ]
)

But LlamaIndex doesn't pass ImageBlocks in assistant messages to the API

### Reason

This capability is only available in GPT-4.1 and not in other models like GPT-4o or GPT-4o-mini, so the implementation would need to be model-aware.

### Value of Feature

This feature would enable more interactive multi-modal conversations where both the user and assistant can share visual information. Use cases include:
Agents that can show visual explanations of concepts
Chatbots that can share diagrams, charts, or images from a knowledge base
Educational applications where the assistant can provide visual examples
Technical support scenarios where the assistant can share reference images
run-llama/llama_index#18478.title: [Feature Request] Support for Assistant-Role Images with GPT-4.1 API

run-llama/llama_index#18479.body:
### Question Validation

- [x] I have searched both the documentation and discord for an answer.

### Question

I've reviewed all the documentation and LlamaIndex's repos and followed their approach of Updating Prompts as follows:
```
react_system_header_str = """\

You are designed to help with database related questions as you are a MySQL expert.

## Tools
You have access to a wide variety of tools. You are responsible for using
the tools in any sequence you deem appropriate to complete the task at hand.
This may require breaking the task into subtasks and using different tools
to complete each subtask.

You have access to the following tools:
{tool_desc}

## Output Format
To answer the question, please use the following format.

\```
Thought: I need to use a tool to help me answer the question.
Action: tool name (one of {tool_names}) if using a tool.
Action Input: the input to the tool, in a JSON format representing the kwargs (e.g. {{"input": "hello world", "num_beams": 5}})
\```

Please ALWAYS start with a Thought.

Please use a valid JSON format for the Action Input. Do NOT do this {{'input': 'hello world', 'num_beams': 5}}.

If this format is used, the user will respond in the following format:

\```
Observation: tool response
\```

You should keep repeating the above format until you have enough information
to answer the question without using any more tools. At that point, you MUST respond
in the one of the following two formats:

\```
Thought: I can answer without using any more tools.
Answer: [your answer here]
\```

\```
Thought: I cannot answer the question with the provided tools.
Answer: Sorry, I cannot answer your query.
\```

## Additional Important Rules
- Always start your answer by `NAMASTE`

## Current Conversation
Below is the current conversation consisting of interleaving human and assistant messages.

"""
react_system_prompt = PromptTemplate(react_system_header_str)
agent.update_prompts({"react_header": react_system_prompt})
print(agent.get_prompts())
```

But Still when I print the prompts by using get_prompts() method it still prints the default Prompt and also it does not follows my instructions passed in the custom prompt, So that assures me that my custom prompt is not getting passed to the agent or LLM.


Can Someone help me around this?

run-llama/llama_index#18547.body:
### Bug Description

Hi, the Hugging Face conversational API was deprecated in 2024, it appears that it has now been removed in favor of the `text-generation` task. I started a related discussion with Hugging Face in their notebooks for their Agent course. https://huggingface.co/agents-course/notebooks/discussions/82

When you have code that uses chat you get an error like this: `huggingface_hub.errors.HfHubHTTPError: 404 Client Error: Not Found for url: https://router.huggingface.co/hf-inference/models/Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct/v1/chat/completions`

In the comments for the HuggingFaceInferenceAPI it mentions

```python
class HuggingFaceInferenceAPI(FunctionCallingLLM):
    """
    Wrapper on the Hugging Face's Inference API.

    Overview of the design:
    - Synchronous uses InferenceClient, asynchronous uses AsyncInferenceClient
    - chat uses the conversational task: https://huggingface.co/tasks/conversational
    - complete uses the text generation task: https://huggingface.co/tasks/text-generation

    Note: some models that support the text generation task can leverage Hugging
    Face's optimized deployment toolkit called text-generation-inference (TGI).
    Use InferenceClient.get_model_status to check if TGI is being used.

    Relevant links:
    - General Docs: https://huggingface.co/docs/api-inference/index
    - API Docs: https://huggingface.co/docs/huggingface_hub/main/en/package_reference/inference_client
    - Source: https://github.com/huggingface/huggingface_hub/tree/main/src/huggingface_hub/inference
    """
```

Note that the url `https://huggingface.co/tasks/conversational` also returns a 404 indicating that HF has likely fully removed the task.

A work around that worked for me was to specify the task in the HuggingFaceInferenceAPI constructor, like so.

```python
llm = HuggingFaceInferenceAPI(
    token=hf_token, 
    model="Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct",
    task="text-generation"
)
```

Here's a full example that only works when the task is set to `text-generation`

**requirements.txt**
```
llama-index
datasets
llama-index-callbacks-arize-phoenix

```

**.env**
```ini
HF_TOKEN=<your token>
```

**load_data.py**
```python
from datasets import load_dataset
from pathlib import Path

dataset = load_dataset(path="dvilasuero/finepersonas-v0.1-tiny", split="train")

Path("data").mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
for i, persona in enumerate(dataset):
    with open(Path("data") / f"persona_{i}.txt", "w", encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write(persona["persona"])
```

**generate_index.py**
```python
# Load files from a directory
from llama_index.core import SimpleDirectoryReader
# Embedding actions
from llama_index.embeddings.huggingface import HuggingFaceEmbedding
from llama_index.core.node_parser import SentenceSplitter
from llama_index.core.ingestion import IngestionPipeline
# Vector storage
import chromadb
from llama_index.vector_stores.chroma import ChromaVectorStore

reader = SimpleDirectoryReader(input_dir="data")
documents = reader.load_data()
print(f"Document count: {len(documents)}")

db = chromadb.PersistentClient(path="./persona_chroma_db")
chroma_collection = db.get_or_create_collection(name="personas")
vector_store = ChromaVectorStore(chroma_collection=chroma_collection)

pipeline = IngestionPipeline(
    transformations=[
        SentenceSplitter(),
        HuggingFaceEmbedding(model_name="BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5"),
    ],
    vector_store=vector_store,
)

nodes = pipeline.run(documents=documents)
print(f"nodes count: {len(nodes)}")
```

**query_index.py** (This is the one that requires the `task="text-generation"` line)
```python
import os
import chromadb
from llama_index.vector_stores.chroma import ChromaVectorStore
from llama_index.llms.huggingface_api import HuggingFaceInferenceAPI
import nest_asyncio
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex
from llama_index.embeddings.huggingface import HuggingFaceEmbedding

from dotenv import load_dotenv

# Load the .env file
load_dotenv()

# Retrieve HF_TOKEN from the environment variables
hf_token = os.getenv("HF_TOKEN")

db = chromadb.PersistentClient(path="./persona_chroma_db")
chroma_collection = db.get_or_create_collection(name="personas")
vector_store = ChromaVectorStore(chroma_collection=chroma_collection)

embed_model = HuggingFaceEmbedding(model_name="BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5")
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_vector_store(
    vector_store=vector_store, embed_model=embed_model
)

nest_asyncio.apply()  # This is needed to run the query engine
llm = HuggingFaceInferenceAPI(
    token=hf_token, 
    model="Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct",
    task="text-generation"
)
query_engine = index.as_query_engine(
    llm=llm,
    response_mode="tree_summarize"
)
response = query_engine.query(
    "Respond using a persona that describes author and travel experiences?"
)

print(f"Response: {response}")
```

### Version

latest from PyPy

### Steps to Reproduce

Create a HuggingFaceInferenceAPI instance without setting the task. Use the inference API with a query engine. A 404 will occur when trying to access the conversational API for the model.

### Relevant Logs/Tracbacks

```shell

```
run-llama/llama_index#18547.title: [Bug]: Hugging Face conversational API returns 404

run-llama/llama_index#18550.body:
### Bug Description

When using CohereEmbedding with a custom endpoint (e.g. Azure AI Foundry) and in an IngestionPipeline, the instance will be serialized since the run method uses multiprocessing. The spawned processes don't retain the custom base_url and fail with invalid API key errors, as they now try to access the original Cohere API.


I'm about to create a PR for this issue.

### Version

0.12.33

### Steps to Reproduce

vector_store=<MY_VECTOR_STORE>
documents=<MY_LIST_OF_DOCS>

embedding = CohereEmbedding(api_key="<MY_API_KEY>", base_url="<MY_API_ENDPOINT>", input_type="search_document")

ingestion_pipeline = IngestionPipeline(transformations=[embedding], vector_store=vector_store)
ingestion_pipeline.run(documents=documents, num_workers=os.cpu_count(), show_progress=True)

### Relevant Logs/Tracbacks

```shell

```
run-llama/llama_index#18550.title: [Bug]: CohereEmbedding fails for custom base_url when used in multiprocessing

run-llama/llama_index#18580.body:
### Feature Description

#### Introduction
The `MetadataFilter` and `MetadataFilters` are crucial in building a robust and sophisticated RAG system, enabling different contexts and different sets of sources to be used in retrieval. These filters are constructed using `FilterConditions` and `FilterOperators`. However, not all `FilterConditions` and `FilterOperators` are supported for all types of Vector Stores.

#### Feature Request
The existing parameters `FilterOperator.IS_EMPTY` is not supported for the `PGVectorStore`. This is currently supported with Weaviate, and some other vector stores. The added support of these parameters to `PGVectorStore` would greatly help developers building RAG with a PostgreSQL database.

#### Reason

**LlamaIndex** could support this feature. It is already supported among a few vector stores, but not `PGVectorStore`. Existing approaches have failed since the conversion to SQL operation requires `IS NULL`, which fundamentally is not integrated in this package.

#### Value of Feature

This feature is valuable because it would enable RAG systems using PostgreSQL to filter metadata for when specific values are null. This is a core feature of database operations, and PostgreSQL is a very common database.
run-llama/llama_index#18580.title: [Feature Request][Bug]: PGVectorStore Support for Null Filtering

run-llama/llama_index#18583.body:
### Bug Description

When inserting and trying to delete a document through the VectorStoreIndex, if the vector_store implementation has its own id's it returns on insert, delete always fails with a KeyError.









### Version

0.12.25

### Steps to Reproduce

- [Inserting code](https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index/blob/355601917eaa0271cb244d669e091bce95206298/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/indices/vector_store/base.py#L233) gets new id's from the `vector_store` implementation and [adds them to the index struct](https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index/blob/355601917eaa0271cb244d669e091bce95206298/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/indices/vector_store/base.py#L243)
- New node is [added to the dict](https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index/blob/355601917eaa0271cb244d669e091bce95206298/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/data_structs/data_structs.py#L193C9-L193C17) with the `vector_id` being what is from the vector_store implementation, while `node_id` is from the document.
- On delete_ref_doc calls, [while removing from the index_struct](https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index/blob/355601917eaa0271cb244d669e091bce95206298/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/indices/vector_store/base.py#L406), ref docs info is fetched unsing the [index_struct node dict values](https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index/blob/355601917eaa0271cb244d669e091bce95206298/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/indices/vector_store/base.py#L419C1-L420C1), and then tries to [delete from the index_struct nodes dict](https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index/blob/355601917eaa0271cb244d669e091bce95206298/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/indices/vector_store/base.py#L392C1-L392C21) using the [`node_id`](https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index/blob/355601917eaa0271cb244d669e091bce95206298/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/data_structs/data_structs.py#L208) instead of the `vector_id`, which will always cause a `KeyError` to be thrown.

### Relevant Logs/Tracbacks

```shell

```
run-llama/llama_index#18583.title: [Bug]: VectorStoreIndex delete_ref_doc always fails on index_struct.delete

run-llama/llama_index#18597.body:
### Bug Description

The current implementation for counting/checking `max_iterations` in the `ReAct Agent` reasoning steps/loop only checks for an exact match between the number of `iterations` and the `max_iterations` e.g. https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index/blob/main/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/agent/react/step.py#L438 

```python
  if len(current_reasoning) == 0:
      raise ValueError("No reasoning steps were taken.")
  elif len(current_reasoning) == self._max_iterations:
      raise ValueError("Reached max iterations.")
```
But it seems both `ActionReasoningStep` and `ObservationReasoningStep` are counted in one iteration and this means that >1 steps can occur in one iteration -> meaning this can skip above but not exactly meet `max_iterations`. 


For example, below is a log output of an instance where the length of `current_reasoning` went over the `max_iterations` but did not trigger a stop due to `max_iterations` being reached:
The `current_reasoning` object is a list of `Action and Observation steps`:
```Thought: The current language of the user is: English. I need to use a tool to help me answer the question.
Action: tool
Action Input: {'query': '...'}
Observation: Error: No such tool named `tool`.
current_reasoning=[ActionReasoningStep(thought='...'}), ObservationReasoningStep(observation='Error: No such tool named `tool`.', return_direct=False), ObservationReasoningStep(observation='Error: No such tool named `tool`.', ObservationReasoningStep(observation='Error: No such tool named `tool`.',] of length 4 and max self._max_iterations=3
```

**This did not raise the max_iterations error**

I've omitted actual queries as it relates to a client project.  But this has been observed a number of times - where in a single `ReAct Agent loop` - there are > 1 steps that mean the subsequent calculation of the current number of reasoning steps. 


It seems this logic is expecting this counter to go up in increments of 1? 

Either way, this check for max_iterations should really be changed to `>=` rather than `==`.

The main problem here is if the number of `reasoning_steps` goes above `max_iterations` the loop can become infinite as the ReAct Agent may not figure out how to complete the task at hand.



### Version

0.12.23.post2

### Steps to Reproduce

As the ReAct agent is effectively an LLM call which may or may not iterate upon its own observations/actions - it is difficult to reproduce deterministically. 

The easiest way to see this problematic behaviour is to set the `max_iterations` to a low number e.g. `2`  and provide multiple tools with instructions to encourage the use of all. Effectively you just need an iteration to involve multiple actions and observations. 


```python
from llama_index.llms.openai import OpenAI
from llama_index.core.agent import ReActAgent

def multiply(a: int, b: int) -> int:
    """Multiply two integers and returns the result integer"""
    return a * b


def add(a: int, b: int) -> int:
    """Add two integers and returns the result integer"""
    return a + b


ReActAgent.from_tools(
            tools=[multiply, add],
            verbose=True,
            max_iterations=2,
        )
```

### Relevant Logs/Tracbacks

```shell

```
run-llama/llama_index#18597.title: [Bug]: ReAct Agent Max Iterations Count Skips Over Check

run-llama/llama_index#18640.body:
### Documentation Issue Description

### Description:
Requesting that changelog updates be automatically included in GitHub release notes. This will help consuming parties more easily track new features and breaking changes when evaluating package updates.
  
### Proposed Solution:
Implement a `.github/release.yml` file to automatically generate release notes. This file can exclude certain authors (e.g., dependabot, pre-commit-ci) from the changelog.
  
#### Example:
```yaml
# .github/release.yml

changelog:
  exclude:
    authors:
      - dependabot
      - pre-commit-ci
```

### Alternative Solution
Implement the [`CSchoel/release-notes-from-changelog@v1`](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/create-release-notes-from-changelog) within [`.github/publish_release.yml`](https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index/blob/main/.github/workflows/publish_release.yml) to include `CHANGELOG.md` updates on release.

### Reference:
For more details, see [GitHub's documentation on automatically generated release notes.](https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/releasing-projects-on-github/automatically-generated-release-notes)

### Documentation Link

https://docs.llamaindex.ai/en/stable/CHANGELOG/
run-llama/llama_index#18640.title: [Documentation]: Automate Release Notes

run-llama/llama_index#18652.body:
### Bug Description

Hallo everyone,
I have encountered a bug when streaming my response to my frontend, where the response_gen generator would already be done before I start streaming it. This results in nothing being streamed from my Backend to the frontend. 
I am using FastAPI and Opentelemetry for tracing. (I kind of have the suspicion that everything works fine when I am not using tracing.)

I followed the [LlamaIndex Docs](https://docs.llamaindex.ai/en/stable/module_guides/deploying/query_engine/streaming/) way to implement Streaming
Here is my simplified code:
```python
class ChatRequest(BaseModel):
    """
    Define chat request model.
    """

    query: str
    chat_history: list[Message] = []


@api_router.post("/chat")
def chat(request: Request, payload: ChatRequest):
    """
    Define chat request handler.
    """

    chat_history = get_chat_history(payload.chat_history)
    system_prompt = "..."
    tools = []

    session_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    with using_session(session_id):
        chat_agent = OpenAIAgent.from_tools(
            tools=tools,
            chat_history=chat_history,
            system_prompt=system_prompt,
        )
        chat_response = chat_agent.stream_chat(
            q=payload.query,
        )

        response_generator = generate(chat_response=chat_response)

        return StreamingResponse(
            response_generator,
            headers={
                "Content-Type": "text/event-stream",
                "Cache-Control": "no-transform",
                "X-Accel-Buffering": "no",
            },
        )


def generate(chat_response: StreamingAgentChatResponse):
    """Generator for streaming response."""
    # Here it sometimes happens that chat_respone.is_done is True, because
    # StreamingAgentChatResponse.write_response_to_history() has already been called
    # This means, that the response is already done generating before I start Streaming
    # So when Iterating over chat_response.response_gen would result in an empty response
    _logger.info(f"LLM is done generating before we start streaming: {chat_response.is_done}")
    for chunk in chat_response.response_gen:
        response_chunk = json.dumps({"type": "query_response", "content": chunk})
        yield f"{response_chunk}\n"

```

I came up with an easy workaround:
``` python
def generate(chat_response: StreamingAgentChatResponse):
    """Generator for streaming response."""

    # Stream LLM response if it is not already done generating
    if not chat_response.is_done:
        for chunk in chat_response.response_gen:
            response_chunk = json.dumps({"type": "query_response", "content": chunk})
            yield f"{response_chunk}\n"

    else:
        # Rare case on small responses:
        # Stream from llm is already done before we start Streaming
        # Iterating over chat_response.response_gen would result in an empty response
        _logger.info("LLM is done generating before we start streaming")
        last_response = chat_response.response
        response_chunk = json.dumps(
            {"type": "query_response", "content": last_response}
        )
        yield f"{response_chunk}\n"
```

### Version

0.12.31

### Steps to Reproduce

Implement a workflow like described.

Ask queries that result in really short or one sentence answers. 
Repeat some times until the bug appears
The bug should appear if the logger 
```python
_logger.info(f"LLM is done generating before we start streaming: {chat_response.is_done}")
```
prints "LLM is done generating before we start streaming: True"

### Relevant Logs/Tracbacks

```shell

```
run-llama/llama_index#18652.title: [Bug]: StreamingAgentChatResponse.response_gen does not yield on short responses
